pulse: US equities pushed to fresh record highs Wednesday as Trump extended the Iran ceasefire indefinitely, lifting tech stocks and the S&P 500 above 7,100. The Strait of Hormuz remains contested with Iran seizing third-party vessels, keeping Brent above $100 and VIX near 20. Tesla Q1 EPS beat but capex guidance raised $5bn, sending shares volatile after the bell. Warsh pledged Fed independence at his Senate confirmation hearing.

movers.name: TSLA
movers.reason: Q1 EPS beat 41c vs 37c est; gains pared after capex raised $5bn to $25bn guidance

movers.name: NDX
movers.reason: Tech sector led risk-on rally on ceasefire extension; US tech +11% in April

movers.name: Brent
movers.reason: Iran seized two more ships in Strait of Hormuz keeping supply risk premium elevated

movers.name: IBEX35
movers.reason: Spanish blue chips near all-time highs on domestic demand and ECB rate cuts

stocks: Singapore-listed; no close data for Apr 22
Check SGX or US listing for latest close
portfolio: US equity positions in strong shape with SPX and NDX at record highs; April tech rally adding roughly 11% to US tech holdings. IBEX35 is Europe standout near all-time highs on 2.3% Spanish GDP forecast and ECB accommodation. Gold at $4752 benefits long positions via war-premium bid. Brent above $100 supports oil exposure but ceasefire extension introduces downside risk if Hormuz resolves. BTC near $78k consolidating with constructive institutional flows. SGD caution: MAS tightened NEER in April; Singapore Q1 GDP contracted 0.3% so STI may face selective pressure.

alerts.text: Iran seized MSC Francesca and Epaminondas in Strait of Hormuz on Apr 22 despite ceasefire - Brent above $100
Tesla capex guidance raised $5bn to $25bn for 2026 - FCF pressure; AH gains reversed
Warsh Fed nomination: pledged independence; DOJ Powell probe complicates Republican Senate support
Singapore Q1 2026 GDP -0.3% q/q; MAS tightened NEER in April review; core CPI 1.5-2.5%
Anthropic Mythos breached on launch day via vendor URL guessing - enterprise security concern
IMF cut 2026 global growth to 3.1% (shadow of US-Iran war); headline inflation to rise modestly

news.title: Trump Extends Iran Ceasefire Indefinitely as Hormuz Tensions Persist
news.text: President Trump extended the US-Iran ceasefire at Pakistan's request, giving Tehran time to present a unified proposal. Iran seized at least two commercial vessels on April 22, with the White House stating non-US ship seizures do not constitute a ceasefire violation. Iran's parliamentary speaker called the US naval blockade itself a violation.

news.title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq Hit Fresh Records on Tech Rally
news.text: The S&P 500 rose 1.05% to 7,137.90 and the Nasdaq gained 1.64% to 24,657.57 on Wednesday April 22, both closing at record highs. US tech is up roughly 11% for April as markets price in de-escalation from the ceasefire extension.

news.title: Tesla Q1 2026: EPS Beat, Revenue Miss, Capex Shock
news.text: Tesla reported Q1 2026 EPS of 41 cents adjusted, beating the 37-cent estimate, on revenue of $22.39 billion versus the $22.64 billion consensus. Deliveries of 358,023 missed the 365,645 estimate. Shares rose 4% after hours then pared gains after Tesla raised full-year capex by $5 billion to $25 billion.

news.title: Kevin Warsh Vows Fed Independence at Senate Hearing
news.text: Trump Fed chair nominee Kevin Warsh testified April 21, pledging he would not be a sock puppet for the president, while arguing the Fed may cut rates without stoking inflation given AI productivity gains. Confirmation is uncertain as a DOJ investigation into current Fed Chair Powell is complicating Republican support.

news.title: IMF Cuts 2026 Global Growth Forecast to 3.1%
news.text: The IMF April 14 World Economic Outlook projects global growth at 3.1% in 2026 and 3.2% in 2027, below recent outcomes and well under the pre-pandemic 3.7% average. The base case assumes a limited Middle East conflict with a 19% rise in energy prices.

news.title: Brent Crude Above $100 as Hormuz Shipping Attacks Continue
news.text: Brent crude rose 3.30% to $101.73 per barrel on April 22 after Iran attacked at least three commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz, which handles roughly 20% of global oil trade. The US naval blockade continues alongside the formal ceasefire.

news.title: The Economist: Can Five Men Be Trusted With AI?
news.text: The Economist April 18-24 cover story The Mythos Moment questions whether five tech CEOs can be trusted with transformative AI power, arguing America's laissez-faire governance approach is no longer politically tenable or strategically wise.

news.title: Amazon Commits Another $25 Billion to Anthropic
news.text: Amazon announced April 20 it will invest up to an additional $25 billion in Anthropic as part of an expanded AI infrastructure deal, deepening the AWS cloud-AI partnership. Anthropic is approaching $19 billion in annualized revenue.

news.title: Singapore Q1 GDP Contracts 0.3%, MAS Tightens NEER
news.text: Singapore's economy shrank 0.3% q/q in Q1 2026 after 1.3% growth the prior quarter, as higher energy costs and weaker global demand weighed on the export-dependent city-state. MAS in its April review increased the NEER appreciation rate with core inflation projected at 1.5-2.5%.

news.title: IBEX35 Near All-Time High as Spain Outperforms Europe
news.text: Spain's IBEX35 is near record territory having gained 44% over twelve months. European Commission forecasts Spanish GDP at 2.3% for 2026 with unemployment set to fall below 10%, supported by domestic demand and ECB rate cuts. Spanish banks contributed roughly 70% of IBEX gains.

news.title: RFK Jr. Now Recommends Measles Vaccine for Every Child
news.text: US Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. said April 22 his department advises every child receive the measles vaccine, a marked shift from his earlier personal-choice framing, following a surge in measles cases and pressure from pediatric health groups.

news.title: Companies Warn Iran War Driving Up Costs Globally
news.text: Consumer goods, travel, and mining companies warned April 22 that the US-Israeli conflict with Iran is driving up costs, disrupting supply chains, and denting consumer confidence globally, per Business Times Singapore reporting.

news.title: SGX-Nasdaq Dual-Listing Bridge Advances
news.text: Singapore Exchange is progressing on a dual-listing bridge with Nasdaq allowing companies to tap liquidity in two time zones simultaneously, part of MAS multi-year push to attract issuers and revitalize SGX capital markets.

news.title: FC Barcelona Wins Record Seventh Consecutive La Liga
news.text: FC Barcelona secured a record seventh consecutive La Liga championship in April 2026, a commercial milestone given the club's ongoing debt restructuring and global brand monetization strategy.

news.title: OECD: Spain Among Top 10 for Labor Tax Burden
news.text: An OECD report in April ranked Spain among the ten member states with the highest taxes on labor. El Economista reported this as a structural competitiveness headwind despite strong near-term GDP growth.

day_ahead: Thursday April 23 events: US initial jobless claims (~215k consensus, 8:30am ET). Multiple Fed speakers expected post-Warsh hearing. Sant Jordi Day in Catalonia. Singapore Open golf at Sentosa Golf Club (April 23-26). TIME100 summit in New York. Iran peace talk watch - Tehran not yet decided on new talks this week. Brent crude and VIX tracking Hormuz headlines.

ai_news.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Mythos 5 - Breached on Day One
ai_news.text: Anthropic released Claude Mythos 5, a ten-trillion-parameter model for advanced cybersecurity and coding, but the launch was marred by unauthorized users accessing Mythos Preview on launch day by guessing a vendor environment URL. Anthropic confirmed it is investigating.

ai_news.title: Anthropic Secures Multi-Gigawatt TPU Deal with Alphabet and Broadcom
ai_news.text: Anthropic announced April 22 that starting in 2027 it will deploy multiple gigawatts of next-generation TPU computing from an Alphabet-Broadcom joint venture. Broadcom expects its custom AI chip business to exceed $100 billion annually by end of 2027.

ai_news.title: Google Signs Multibillion-Dollar Deal with Mira Murati Thinking Machines Lab
ai_news.text: Google deepened ties with Thinking Machines Lab, the startup from former OpenAI executive Mira Murati, through a new multibillion-dollar agreement announced April 22 for expanded Google Cloud AI infrastructure access.

ai_news.title: OpenAI Tops $25 Billion Revenue, Eyes Late-2026 IPO
ai_news.text: OpenAI has surpassed $25 billion in annualized revenue and is taking early steps toward a public listing potentially as soon as late 2026, while Anthropic approaches $19 billion in annualized revenue.

ai_news.title: Anthropic Tests Removing Claude Code from Pro Tier
ai_news.text: Anthropic is testing removal of Claude Code from its Pro subscription for roughly 2% of new signups, citing changed usage patterns since the plan was designed. Existing Pro and Max subscribers are not affected.
